data.title: Not worth the hype. Beware!!!

Gave Blackline a shot because of their marketing on my feed and i can tell you their towels are not a big as you think they are or in comparison to other manufactuers. Contacted customer service which can only be done by email to see if i could exchange my unopened towels for the bigger more expensive and they said no all sales final. Be careful ordering from this company. Look at more reputable companies with honest marketing and better customer service.

data.title: Extremely Disappointed – Not Worth the Hype
data.text: I bought this towel expecting “#1 rated” performance, but it completely missed the mark. It looked plush out of the package, but in actual use, it left behind water streaks and lint all over my black paint. For something claiming to be 1300 GSM, it doesn’t absorb nearly as much as my cheaper drying towels. After one wash, it lost its softness and started shedding fibers like crazy. Save your money—this is all hype and no quality. Definitely not a premium product.

data.title: Honest review
data.text: This is an honest review that was not sponsored!I want to start off my saying my car is brand new  so it doesn't have any ceramic coating. I bought this with high hopes because I was influenced on social media. Come to find out it's just the same as every other drying towel. There is nothing really special about it..As you can see on my video + pictures it leaves streak marks on any swipe. I eventually had to use my OG drying towel.I guess the only positive from this product is the size and it's somewhat soft but that's pretty much it. There's a bit of weight to it making it hard to dry the whole car. It also wasn't easy trying to ring it out. Over all disappointed in this product I will not be keeping it.

data.title: This towel is not a good product!!!
data.text: The towel left my car looking horrible!!! It does not absorb as well as the I hoped it would. This towel had my car looking worse than what it did before I washed it. Not worth having it in my detail collection.

data.title: Good for one use
data.text: First time I used this it worked great. Cared for it as specified on packaging but the next use was not so hot.  Quickly absorbed and would not hold anymore moisture after just doing the hood of the car. Streaked the rest of the vehicle after that.

data.title: Dries vehicle great, BUT TOWEL WILL FALL APART!!
data.text: Towel dries very very well. One pass per panel and it’s dry to the touch with zero streaking. However, after 3 uses the towel is now starting to fall apart very easily at the corners. Dries fantastic, but beware it will fall apart after only 3 uses. Very disappointed.

data.title: Seams are weak, but it does the job.
data.text: I’ve had this towel for a couple months and I will say that it’s very effective drying my car after a wash. I can dry almost my entire little SUV with it. The size is good, not too large, not too small. It does start getting heavy and a bit cumbersome as it gets wetter but that’s just the towel doing its job of holding water.I dropped off three stars on my review because one of the seams on it has already given up and split, see photo. It’s past the return period. This is not what I’d expect from a product of this price point; the seams should last years, not months. I’m not an every-weekend car washer so I’ve only wash the towel a few times in the time I’ve owned it. I just hope that the seam doesn’t completely unravel.

data.title: Decent, but is not quite as advertised
data.text: The towel fresh out of the package leaves streaks behind, meaning it does not pick up water like they show in the videos. It may pick all the water up in one pass if whatever you’re drying is ceramic coated. It does work better than the best of what you will find in automotive sections of stores and auto parts stores, but don’t expect to get the results they advertise. I haven’t experimented enough with it yet, but dragging the towel across the surface of a wet car feels and sounds similar to using a bathroom towel. Audibly sounds like it’s scratching, but I have not checked for any new scratches quite yet. I might not use it again for that reason.

data.title: Stitching in raveling

data.title: Not worth the price

data.title: Hard to dry
data.text: Once when towel gets wet it is very hard to dry, even the spin cycle on washing machine would not take the water out, I know it’s made to hold water but there has to be a easier way.

data.title: Good for 1 car, but is it truly 1300GSM?
data.text: Compared to other towels I've purchased, the 1300GSM claim seems to be under what my other drying towels feel like. I don't have any way to scientifically test this, but it is a little disappointing.Overall, the fabric is good enough to fully dry off one car, but when I needed to do more than 1 sedan-sized vehicle, the towel started struggling, which adds to my assumption that the 1300GSM claim may be wrong. Be wary.

data.title: Apparently not very durable.
data.text: Overall, this towel lives up to the hype. Very large, streak-free drying, and super absorbent. However, my 3-star review is based on the fact that I was only able to use this towel once before it started to fray and the stitching came apart. While these towels are generally affordable, it's still a purchase that I made with hopes of having a new favorite drying towel that would last a while, and I'm very disappointed that I was only able to get one use out of it before it started to come apart. Haven't even washed it yet, simply used it to dry a vehicle and hung it on a drying rack to air dry when I noticed the threads coming out. Will add a picture or two later to explain, but overall not super thrilled with it.

data.title: Towel is Coming Apart
data.text: Towel is nice, but mine is already separating at the seams where the two pieces are sewn together.  It only has a 30 day warranty and I’ve had mine for 3 months.  It’s been used like 4 times for personal use.  Their website says they stand behind their product but in my opinion the product warranty should be on a case by case basis.

data.title: Leaves water streaks
data.text: My friend told me it didn’t leave water streaks when he uses it and so did a lot of the reviews. But I used it on my black car and on the first wipe out of package, left a water streak, I used it to try my whole car, it’s a very small suv. And towards the end of drying it, starting leaving more water streaks, I used some other smaller microfiber towels to go over the car again in the spots it left the water streaks. It’s not a bad towel, to me the material felt a little rough, but didn’t scratch my car. Still going to use it, better than using the one I was using before.

data.title: The weight is very heavy.
data.text: It works good, takes the water out but the weight of this towel is very heavy, also it does take long time to dry out, as you can’t dry this in machine. Other than that it works good.

data.title: Good towel. Not perfect. If you expect a single pass spotless dry, this misses a bit.
data.text: I bought the Blackline drying towel. For my first pull on my hood after a wash. It works. I dried the entire car with one towel. Absorption is strong and it feels soft on the paint.The performance falls short of the hype. It leaves light streaks in certain areas. You can see it in my video. I had to make a second pass on panels to get a clean finish.What I noticed:• Great water pickup on the first pass• Safe on paint with no scratching• Large size makes drying fast• Streaking on darker panels• Requires a quick follow up wipe in spotsGood towel. Not perfect. If you expect a single pass spotless dry, this misses a bit. If you are fine doing a quick second wipe, it does the job well.

data.title: Need more than two of these
data.text: Good towel for drying a small car. Not the super soaker it is made out to be in the listing and some reviews. I used it on a large SUV and I think I’ll need 3x to dry properly without the towel saturating.

I have purchased the Sminder two pack that has given me a similar performance at a lower cost. My vehicle is ceramic coated and water sheds off easily. I would purchase again if it was less expensive.

data.title: Works great maybe quality issues.
data.text: First the product does work and works well. It dried my entire Large sedan (bmw m5) and left no streaks or water spots so if you want the product get it BUTTT its not perfect thats why I chose 4 stars. Idk if its a quality control issue or what but I had about 5 to 6 loose strings coming from the towel that I had to cut off. The appeard to be extra where after sewing it was ment to be cut but wasn't not a major deal but I wonder how that will effect the long-term of the product and if after some washes if it'll fall apart.TLDR: product works great but some quality control issues.

data.title: Ultimate Drying Power in One Towel
data.text: This towel is an absolute game-changer for car detailing. The size is perfect for drying an entire car without constantly wringing it out, and the absorbency is next-level—it soaks up water like a sponge. It glides smoothly over the surface without leaving any streaks or scratches, and the plush microfiber feels incredibly soft and premium. A must-have for anyone serious about keeping their car spotless.

data.title: Pretty good, take care of it and it’ll take care of you
data.text: I’m a professional detailer for a collision repair shop, I clean cars after they’ve been fixed. We use these a good bit on the cars we do when they’re done being washed, they’re great. Just make sure you wash them separately from other towels that might have chemicals or debris on them, kinda ruins the way it absorbs water. That’s the same that can be said for any towel tho, so if you have rags/towels specific to drying your car you should be washing them separately anyways. Will not scratch your car, pretty simple to use, size makes it easier to dry cars quickly, especially if you’re a smaller person and would typically have to reach across the hood or roof to get it fully dried. Pretty much the standard as far what the texture of a towel should be for using on a car if you’re serious about taking care of your paint, tons of other “microfiber” towels say they won’t scratch and do. I’ve used tons of different kinds of towels and rags, these are by far the best to use for drying. Definitely make sure you clean the car thoroughly tho because you do not want hard objects like rock or dirt to stick to the towel because you will scratch your paint, that will happen with anything you use so just be careful and mindful. Overall, 10/10. If they weren’t as expensive as they are, I’d probably have about a dozen of them for work and a couple for myself

data.title: Better than advertised
data.text: Amazing actually works better than I thought it would. 2021 Shelby GT500 dries the whole car and only feels damn afterwards.

data.title: Best drying towel on the market, hands down.
data.text: Well worth the price.  If you want a great quality drying towel for your car, rv, boat then this is your best bet.  My husband washed our boat and normally he will wax it too since it was in salt water, but this time he was so. pleased with how it looked after drying he skipped the waxing part.  You will not have streaks or spots after using the drying towel.

data.title: Doubts about this towel...
data.text: I had doubts about this product, seeing all those adds about going over the hood one time with no streaks and dries fast. But I bought one and to my surprise, it did it's job and I got rid of the long time Chamois which I thought was the best drying towel  of all time. This Blackline towel was a great invention, it usually took me about 20 minutes to finish drying with the Chamois, but now it took me about 5 minutes I was stun. I am a satisfied customer and you will be very happy when you getthis for your car, I guarantee!!!!!

data.title: No Drying Towel is Better
data.text: This towel is fantastic, dried the entire vehicle and still didn’t seem soaked. Didn’t have to wring it out the entire drying process. Left no streaks and sucked up every drop of water. My new go to, spend the extra money and get this.

data.title: Detailing or Simple wipe downs, this Towel does it all!
data.text: Car detailing or simple wash and go! This towel is perfect for it all! I can reach all of the crevices, I can dry all of my windows without streaks. It's large enough to get complete both of my cars. Super durable, easy to clean, very large, thick! It's great to even keep in the car for quick wipe downs.

data.title: Great Product

I was skeptical on this but placed the order. The used it today and AM VERY PLEASED as it does exactly what they say.  It dried my Truck quickly, LEFT NO WATER OR SPOTS on it. I  will be buying another one when needed.

data.title: Best drying towel ever!!
data.text: 10/10 product. Definitely is good as advertised. Love drying my car with it, holds a lot of water, and is very big! It takes me a lot less time to dry my car after a long time of washing. Also the towel is extremely soft on my cars paint and doesn’t leave any micro scratches.

data.title: 5 Stars – Massive, Absorbent, and Exactly as Advertised
data.text: LOVE this towel. The Blackline 24” x 36” Twisted-Loop Car Drying Towel is absolutely as described. It’s large, super absorbent, and made drying our ’25 Escalade Sport Platinum incredibly easy. It handled the size of the vehicle with ease and made quick work of the job without feeling saturated too fast. If you want a quality drying towel that performs like it should, this one delivers. Highly recommend.

data.title: Heavy, Thirsty Towels!
data.text: My husband asked me to order one for each of our vehicles and I must say I am pleased with the quality of this towel. As directed, we washed first with the suggested Blackline detergent. The towels left no residue or streaks and our vehicles are as shiny as the day we purchased. I am hoping the towels hold their own because my husband has burned a considerable amount of money over the years with other vendors. So far, we are happy with this purchase.

data.title: Must have, especially if you have a larger vehicle!
data.text: How have I been drying my car all this time with little microfiber towels?! For my hatchback, after washing (with this particular car I’m avoiding drive through automated washes)  literally just a few swipes with this huge towel and all good to go! I couldn’t imagine trying to dry large truck or van etc with little towels, if you’re into washing/detailing car yourself, please give these towels a try-A+Great Product!

data.title: Overall, highly recommended if you want a towel that actually works.
data.text: The Blackline 24”x36” twisted-loop drying towel is a really good product. It dries fast, leaves no streaks, and doesn’t leave lint behind. The microfiber feels high quality and it’s reusable, so it lasts a long time. One pass is all you need to dry your car clean.

data.title: This towel really works!!
data.text: This towel really works (as my title says!)! I can dry off the morning dew on my entire Sienna van with just this towel...really amazing. It takes a while to dry, but I have another one that I alternate with.
